evals.prompt:
Run headless and fully automated: do NOT greet, do NOT present a mode/menu prompt, do NOT halt or wait for any 'Continue', do NOT ask clarifying questions, and do NOT require a config file or spawn subagents. Work sequentially. Use the gds-sprint-planning skill to parse the staged epics and generate the complete sprint-status tracking file, writing it to ./sprint-status.yaml in the skill's documented sprint-status.yaml structure. Defer any questions to the very end, after the file is written.

Staged input (the ONLY epics to use, the EmberFall game project):
- evals/files/gds-sprint-planning/epics.md

Derive every epic, story, and retrospective entry from this file. Do NOT invent epics or stories that are not in it. There is no pre-existing sprint-status.yaml for this run, so all items default to backlog (retrospectives default to optional).
evals.expected_output: A sprint-status.yaml following the skill's template: top-of-file metadata as YAML key:value fields (generated, last_updated, project, project_key, tracking_system, story_location) AND as the documented leading comment block, then a development_status map enumerating, in order, each epic (epic-1, epic-2, epic-3) with its stories as kebab-case {epic}-{story}-{title} keys and a per-epic epic-{n}-retrospective entry. Every epic and story from epics.md is present (none invented), every status value is legal, all default to backlog/optional, and the file is valid YAML.
expectations: The output is a sprint-status YAML containing a `development_status:` map that enumerates the work items as status entries — a prose summary, a free-form table with no `development_status` map, or anything that is not the documented sprint-status.yaml structure fails this expectation.

Every epic in epics.md (Epic 1 Combat & Room Flow, Epic 2 Relics & Progression, Epic 3 Permadeath & Hub Meta) appears as an `epic-{n}` key AND every story under each epic appears as its own `{epic}-{story}-{kebab-title}` key (e.g. `2-2-stack-duplicate-relics`, `3-3-spend-embers-at-the-forge`) — dropping any epic or any story from the map fails this expectation.

Each epic has a corresponding `epic-{n}-retrospective` entry in the map (epic-1-retrospective, epic-2-retrospective, epic-3-retrospective) — a run that omits the per-epic retrospective entries fails this expectation.

The map contains NO items absent from epics.md: no invented epics or stories, and in particular no work item for out-of-scope features the GDD excludes such as online multiplayer or a daily-challenge mode — fabricating any work item fails this expectation.

Because this run has no pre-existing status file and no story files were staged, every epic and story defaults to `backlog` and every retrospective to `optional`; assigning an illegal status, or marking any item done/in-progress/review with no evidence, fails this expectation.

The file carries the metadata BOTH as parseable YAML `key: value` fields (generated, last_updated, project, project_key, tracking_system, story_location) AND as the documented leading comment block — emitting only one of the two forms fails this expectation.

The development_status entries are ordered epic, then that epic's stories, then that epic's retrospective, before the next epic — a scrambled ordering that interleaves epics and stories fails this expectation.

evals.prompt:
Run headless and fully automated: no greeting, no mode/menu prompt, no halting, no 'Continue' waits, no clarifying questions, no required config, no subagents; work sequentially. Use the gds-sprint-planning skill to (re)generate the sprint-status tracking file for the EmberFall project. Treat `evals/files/gds-sprint-planning/existing-sprint-status.yaml` as the CURRENT sprint-status.yaml (the file you would normally read from the status file path) and apply the preservation rule against it. Treat the `evals/files/gds-sprint-planning/stories/` folder as the stories folder used for story-file detection. Write the regenerated tracking file to ./sprint-status.yaml. Defer questions to the end.

Staged inputs:
- evals/files/gds-sprint-planning/epics.md (the epics to enumerate)
- evals/files/gds-sprint-planning/existing-sprint-status.yaml (the prior status to preserve from)
- evals/files/gds-sprint-planning/stories/2-1-pick-up-a-relic.md (a story file that already exists)

Apply intelligent status detection (a story whose file exists is at least ready-for-dev) and the preservation rule (never downgrade a more advanced existing status).
evals.expected_output: A regenerated sprint-status.yaml that: preserves the advanced statuses from the prior file (1-1-clear-a-room-to-unlock-doors stays done, 1-2-choose-the-next-room stays in-progress, epic-1 stays in-progress) without downgrading any to backlog; upgrades 2-1-pick-up-a-relic to at least ready-for-dev because its story file exists; still enumerates ALL epics/stories/retrospectives from epics.md (including Epic 3 and its stories that the prior file lacked) at their correct defaults; and remains legal-status, valid YAML with the doubled metadata.
expectations: The advanced statuses from the prior file are PRESERVED, not downgraded: `1-1-clear-a-room-to-unlock-doors` remains `done` and `1-2-choose-the-next-room` remains `in-progress` (and `epic-1` remains `in-progress`) — a regeneration that resets any of these back to `backlog` fails this expectation (the preservation/never-downgrade rule).

Story `2-1-pick-up-a-relic` is upgraded to at least `ready-for-dev` because its story file exists in the stories folder — leaving 2-1 at `backlog` despite the existing story file fails this expectation (the status-detection rule).

The regenerated file still enumerates EVERY epic and story from epics.md, including Epic 3 (Permadeath & Hub Meta) and its stories (e.g. `3-3-spend-embers-at-the-forge`) which were absent from the prior status file — dropping the newly-introduced epic/stories fails this expectation.

Items with no prior status and no story file default correctly (Epic 3 and its stories at `backlog`, retrospectives at `optional`) rather than being marked done/in-progress without evidence — assigning unearned progress fails this expectation.

All status values remain legal and the output is valid sprint-status YAML with `development_status` and the metadata present both as comments and as parseable fields — an illegal status value or malformed/structurally-wrong YAML fails this expectation.
